column5: Question

Describe major aspects of the civilizations in regions throughout the world 
prior to European contact including government, religion/belief systems, 
arts/architecture, technology, physical geography, economics, and social 
order.

Investigate the historical origins, central beliefs, and spread of major 
religions and belief systems, including Judaism, Christianity, Islam, Sikhism, 
Hinduism, Buddhism, and Confucianism and Indigenous knowledge and 
belief systems.

Compare and contrast cultural patterns in regions throughout the world,
such as language, religion, and ethnicity.

Discuss how social institutions, including the family, religion, and education, 
influence behavior in different societies in regions throughout the world.

Analyze the characteristics of early civilizations throughout the world
including government, religion/belief systems, arts/architecture, technology,
physical geography, economics, and social order.
Explain the relationship between religion and belief systems and people's understanding of the natural world.
Explain how religion and belief systems shaped the development of civilizations.
Discuss how religion, belief systems, economics, and politics influenced social behavior and were used to maintain social order.
Examine why the diversity of religion and belief systems across cultural, social, political, and economic institutions have been sources of conflict

column3: World History and Civilizations, 5. Global Perspectives
column5:

various civilizations and their consequences.
